channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Meet the Bop House, the internet’s divisive new OnlyFans hype house
item.description: What if the Playboy Mansion was filled with OnlyFans content creators? That’s the pitch for the Bop House, a TikTok page that has gained nearly three million followers since its launch in December.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: SoftBank reveals $2.4 billion loss in Q3
item.description: Japanese technology company SoftBank Group Corp. reported a 369.2 billion yen ($2.4 billion) loss for the fiscal third quarter as it racked up red ink from its Vision Fund investments. That’s compared to a 950 billion yen profit in October-December 2023.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Thomson Reuters scores early win in AI copyright battles in the US
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Thomson Reuters has won an early battle in court over the question of fair use in artificial intelligence-related copyright cases.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Showing memory usage in Linux by process and user
item.description: There are a lot of tools for looking at memory usage on Linux systems. Some are commonly used commands like ps, while others are tools like top that allow you to display system performance stats in various ways.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Grimes rips Elon Musk for using their 4-year-old son as ‘a prop’ in Trump appearances
item.description: MSNBC host Lawrence O'Donnell also said that the little boy's presence in an Oval Office press conference with Trump Tuesday was 'a power play' by the tech billionaire.
item.dc:creator: Martha Ross, The Mercury News

item.title: Apple’s Chinese AI problem (perhaps) solved with Alibaba
item.description: Reflecting the erosion of universality, Apple Intelligence will now be coming to China, but rather than working with a US AI partner, the company will use Chinese-made AI tech from Alibaba.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Looking for love this Valentine's Day? Don't fall for Instagram romance scams
item.description: Facebook and Instagram parent company Meta Platforms is urging users to stay vigilant about “romance scams” ahead of Valentine’s Day, warning of unsolicited messages through its apps and other social media platforms, as well as general text messages.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The Samsung Galaxy S25 Edge has appeared on Geekbench with so-so performance scores – but we're not worried yet
item.description: The Samsung Galaxy S25 Edge has appeared on Geekbench with the same specs as the S25 Ultra, but much lower scores.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: 3 ways Tesla stands to win from Elon Musk’s war on the U.S. government
item.description: Elon Musk has long railed against the U.S. government, saying a crushing number of federal investigations and safety programs have stymied Tesla, his electric car company, and its efforts to create fleets of robotaxis and other self-driving automobiles.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Google Calendar users no longer see default entries for events like Pride, Black History Month
item.description: Google’s online calendar has removed default references for a handful of holidays and cultural events — with users noticing that mentions of Pride and Black History Month, as well as other observances, no longer appear in their desktop and mobile applications.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Paris AI Action Summit: US and UK refuse to sign accord
item.description: The escalating electricity demands of artificial intelligence systems are raising concerns about the technology’s sustainability — but that’s apparently of little concern to the governments of the US and the UK.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Why the Trump administration should uphold this final cyber executive order
item.description: Ilona Cohen, the chief legal and policy officer at HackerOne, explains why President Joe Biden’s new cyber executive order should be a priority. The post Why the Trump administration should uphold this final cyber executive order first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Esports Olympics to debut in Saudi Arabia later than expected in 2027
item.description: LAUSANNE, Switzerland (AP) — The first Olympic Esports Games will be hosted in Riyadh in 2027, two years later than expected when a 12-year deal with Saudi Arabia was signed last year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ukraine's rare earth elements could help keep US military aid flowing
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine has offered to strike a deal with U.S. President Donald Trump for continued American military aid in exchange for developing Ukraine’s mineral industry, which could provide a valuable source of the rare earth elements that are essential for many kinds of technology.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Apple’s security patch highlights the growing security threat
item.description: Apple’s platforms may be more secure by design than others, but that doesn’t make them invulnerable to attack. That’s why every user should install the company’s latest security patch — it fixes a hole Apple says may already have been in active use.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: C++, Go, and Rust gaining popularity – Tiobe
item.description: With Tiobe’s February 2025 rankings, the publisher of the monthly index of programming language popularity notes that fast programming languages such as C++, Go, and Rust are gaining ground.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: News influencers favored Trump over Harris during campaign, study finds
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Americans who followed news influencers during the presidential campaign were more likely to hear positive reports about Donald Trump than they were about Kamala Harris, a study has revealed.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Cisco launches AI Renewals Agent with Mistral AI
item.description: Cisco rolled out its first of many AI agents codeveloped with partner Mistral AI, a genAI and large language model (LLM) startup.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: NetOps teams ready to dump incumbent monitoring tools
item.description: Replacing a network management tool is not a trivial exercise, especially in large and complex networks. It can take months to install and tune a tool so that it is delivering value.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Trump's AI ambition and China's DeepSeek overshadow an AI summit in Paris
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The geopolitics of artificial intelligence will be in focus at a major summit in France where world leaders, executives and experts will hammer out pledges on guiding the development of the rapidly advancing technology.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Never lose cell service again with this space-age satellite tech
item.description: AST SpaceMobile of Texas is developing the world's first global cellular broadband network that can connect directly to your smartphone without special equipment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Latino workers working to overcome a technological divide brought on by automation, AI
item.description: As jobs become more reliant on technology some Latino workers can be left behind due to a lack of digital skills exacerbated by a lack of accessibility.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: New law in Brazil is making students put away their smartphones at school
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian students returned to class this week with a new task: staying away from their smartphones as a new law restricting their use in schools took effect.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: OpenAI looks across US for sites to build its Trump-backed Stargate AI data centers
item.description: OpenAI is scouring the U.S. for sites to build a network of huge data centers to power its artificial intelligence technology, expanding beyond a flagship Texas location and looking across 16 states to accelerate the Stargate project championed by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Nebraska government system shutdown disrupts the Legislature, DMV and other offices
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — A systemwide outage of state goverment computers shut down everything from the work of the Legislature to driver’s licensing offices for several hours Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MARGERY A. BECK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Beware of fake Reddit solutions delivering dangerous malware
item.description: Bad actors are now mimicking Reddit to spread malware that can steal personal information. CyberGuy shares what you need to know about fake Reddit pages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to block your phone from tracking your location
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Smartphones are useful tools for everyday life, but they're privy to nearly everything about you, including all the places you've been — if you let them.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation
